description:
You are acting as a DevOps engineer who has been asked to perform a quick, one-off analysis of an application log that is already present on the system.  
The entire job can be done with only 2–3 shell commands, but *what* those commands are is up to you.

Current state of the system
1. Log directory:  
   /home/user/logs  

2. File already present:  
   /home/user/logs/app.log

   Contents of /home/user/logs/app.log (7 lines, shown here exactly):

   2024-04-01 10:15:32 INFO  Application started  
   2024-04-01 10:16:45 ERROR Failed to connect to database  
   2024-04-01 10:17:02 WARN  Retrying connection  
   2024-04-01 10:17:10 INFO  Connected successfully  
   2024-04-01 10:18:21 ERROR Timeout during query  
   2024-04-01 10:19:01 INFO  Request completed  
   2024-04-01 10:20:11 INFO  Shutdown complete  

(Notice there are exactly two lines whose level field is “ERROR”.)

Task you must accomplish
A. Create a new SQLite database file named  
   /home/user/logs/debug.db

B. Inside that database create a single table called logs with three TEXT columns, in this order:  
      ts      (timestamp)  
      level   (log level)  
      msg     (free-text message)

C. Import every line from /home/user/logs/app.log into that table so that:
   • Column ts contains the full date-time stamp  
   • Column level contains the exact log-level word (INFO / WARN / ERROR)  
   • Column msg contains the remaining text of the line with no leading spaces

D. Run a query that counts how many rows have level='ERROR', then write the
   result to a brand-new file  
   /home/user/logs/error_summary.log

   The output file must contain **exactly one line** and follow **exactly** this format (no extra spaces, no newline at the end):

   ERROR_COUNT:<integer>

   For the sample data supplied the file must end up containing:

   ERROR_COUNT:2

Verification rules used by the grader
1. /home/user/logs/debug.db must exist and be a valid SQLite database.  
2. The database must have a table named logs with columns ts, level, msg (in that order).  
3. SELECT COUNT(*) FROM logs; must return 7.  
4. SELECT COUNT(*) FROM logs WHERE level='ERROR'; must return 2.  
5. /home/user/logs/error_summary.log must exist, be world-readable, and its entire
   content must be the single ASCII string “ERROR_COUNT:2” with no trailing newline.

Hints (do NOT rely on these hints being present at grading time)
• You can achieve everything with the sqlite3 CLI using the “.mode” and “.import” commands followed by a single SQL query whose output you redirect into the summary file.  
• The grading system will run its own queries; it will not just look at console output.

Remember: do not show your commands here, just execute them in the terminal.
truth:
Pre-existing files before the agent starts  
/home/user/logs/app.log  
(contains the seven lines shown in the public statement)

Files/directories that must exist after successful completion
1. /home/user/logs/debug.db  
   • Must be an SQLite 3 database file.  
   • Schema must include exactly one table:  
        CREATE TABLE logs (
            ts   TEXT,
            level TEXT,
            msg  TEXT
        );
   • Row count: 7 total rows, 2 rows where level='ERROR'.

2. /home/user/logs/error_summary.log  
   • Mode: 0644 (rw-r--r--) or more permissive.  
   • Content (no trailing newline):  
     ERROR_COUNT:2

Grader verification steps
1. test -f /home/user/logs/debug.db
2. sqlite3 /home/user/logs/debug.db "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM logs;"  → must print “7”
3. sqlite3 /home/user/logs/debug.db "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM logs WHERE level='ERROR';" → must print “2”
4. test -f /home/user/logs/error_summary.log
5. read -r line < /home/user/logs/error_summary.log; line must equal “ERROR_COUNT:2”

Any deviation (wrong schema, wrong counts, extra newline, etc.) fails the task.
name: task_000000_83a11f06
